Operation 'runInference' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description is too generic and lacks critical details for AI agent decision-making. Enhanced version specifies: (1) what agents are available and their purposes (domain context), (2) required parameters (agent_code), (3) expected input format (image data), (4) detailed output structure (InferenceResult with confidence scores), (5) processing behavior (synchronous). This enables AI agents to understand when to use this operation, what data to provide, and how to interpret results.

data.current_description: Execute AI vision analysis using the specified agent
data.summary_suggestion: Run vision analysis inference with specified agent
data.description_suggestion: Executes AI-powered vision analysis on provided image data using the specified agent (e.g., cleanliness scoring, damage detection, label reading, object counting). Requires agent_code path parameter to identify which analysis agent to use. Accepts image input in request body. Returns InferenceResult containing analysis output, confidence scores, and detected objects/metrics specific to the selected agent. Processing is synchronous and completes within the request timeout.
